explanatory_strength.rationale: Operating snapshot quantifies revenue stability ($54.6B FY2025), margin compression (operating income −44% YoY to $1.5B), and OCF versus GAAP net income, but the competitive-advantage mechanism is absent: classification is moat none and capital_cycle, not franchise or oligopoly pricing power. The levered-stub narrative (deleveraging from $3.1B OCF against $29B debt) is plausible yet indistinguishable from a generic distressed-equity option without peer cost-yield gaps or structural volume drivers Hohn requires.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: FY2025 10-K and Q1 2026 10-Q anchor revenue, margins, cash flow, debt, and share count; calculation proofs are 100% complete across four additive components with clean overlap controls. Gaps remain for Hohn fieldwork: management_facts_2026-07-21.json is empty, AAdvantage standalone economics are judgment-only ($0–$20/sh band), and mid-cycle owner-cash normalization ($4.70/sh OCF proxy) versus mandatory $3.8B capex is not reconciled to maintenance versus growth spend.

downside_control.rationale: Equity downside is not bounded: ~$29B total debt and leases versus $902M cash, capex exceeding operating cash flow, dhando none, and five-question gate 3 fail. Proof-first net financial claims are −$42.67/sh base with an additional cycle/leverage reserve −$9.00/sh; simultaneous earnings trough and credit tightening could force dilutive refinancing before deleveraging completes.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Proof-first component sum is −$9.79/sh base at $15.14 spot; contract base annualized return is null and high case only 8.12% over seven years. Lawrence synthesis 28.48% and bull 35% rely on scenario weights that diverge from filing-locked component economics and fail the ~15% medium-term Hohn bar on conservative proof-first math. Upside/downside screen (~25–30% upside / ~10% downside) is not met at the equity layer.

claims.claim: FY2025 operating cash flow was $3.1 billion (~$4.70 per diluted share) while net income was only $111 million; operating income fell 44% YoY to $1.5 billion on flat revenue near $54.6 billion.

Proof-first additive components sum to −$9.79 per share base (network $33.88 + co-brand option $8.00 − net financial claims $42.67 − cycle reserve $9.00) versus price $15.14, with no double-counting flags.
Classification records moat none, dhando none, and payoff lens levered; total debt and finance leases ~$29.0 billion against $902 million cash at FY2025 year-end.

strongest_counter_explanation: The market correctly prices AAL as a cheap equity option on mid-cycle deleveraging: sustained $3B+ operating cash flow can retire the ~$29B debt stack over a cycle while AAdvantage co-brand mile sales provide high-margin cash not fully separated in filings, making the conservative proof-first component sum too punitive relative to normalized network earning power.
most_important_missing_fact: Standalone AAdvantage co-brand and partner mile-sale cash economics and renewal terms from SEC filings or management disclosure, to test whether the $8 per share base option component is incremental franchise value or already embedded in the $4.70 per share operating-cash-flow proxy used for the network proof.
falsifiers: Trailing four-quarter operating cash flow per share falls below $3.50 for four consecutive quarters without proportional debt reduction below $26 billion
Total debt and lease obligations rise above $32 billion for two consecutive quarters while operating income stays below $2.0 billion
Operating margin turns negative while capital spending remains above $3.5 billion, indicating mandatory fleet renewal without fare power to fund it
specialist_findings: Outside Hohn power zone (0 of 2 required rule matches: capital_cycle archetype, moat none, ls_algo_underlying sleeve). Competitive-advantage audit: no durable franchise or pricing power; U.S. network scale is oligopoly without Hohn-style barriers. Overlap controls are clean—four unique overlap keys, empty double_counting_flags, co-brand option explicitly non-overlapping with network OCF. Valuation-proof audit: all four calculation graphs valid; midcycle_passenger_network applies judgment capitalization 4.5–10.5× on OCF proxy despite capex exceeding OCF; aadvantage_co_brand_option base $5.3B milestone is judgment with probability_basis citing industry precedents only; net_financial_claims filing-locked at −$42.67/sh; cycle_leverage_reserve −$9/sh sizes fuel/recession stress separately. Lawrence synthesis 28.48% diverges materially from proof-first −$9.79/sh. No management fieldwork; ICS 0.0. Hohn entry screen fails on business quality, bounded downside, and absolute return versus ~15% hurdle.
